title: fraxinus oxycarpa var. tamariscifolia
intro: Fraxinus oxycarpa var. tamariscifolia is a variety of the Fraxinus oxycarpa tree, commonly known as the forest ash or Manchurian ash, native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and acting as an antiseptic. Fraxinus oxycarpa var. tamariscifolia is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its bark, leaves, and roots are used in herbal medicine to create various preparations.
